The '''Stenax Massacres''' was a conflict that took place after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Background==
Shortly after the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] established a presence on the [[Stenax]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Stenos/Legends|Stenos]], several Stenaxes traveled off-world and worked as [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] or performed other duties for little pay while experiencing little interaction with others. This behavior was part of a plot by the Stenaxes to study other [[Species/Legends|species]] and determine which were most susceptible to conquest. Placing operatives on [[Keyorin|Hunter's World]], [[Kabray]], [[Delrakkin/Legends|Delrakkin]], [[Cilpar]], and other [[Planet/Legends|planets]], they learned to use the advanced technologies found on those worlds while awaiting a signal to attack. A few of the Stenaxes lived as [[Merchant/Legends|traders]], secretly evaluating those they dealt with as opponents. None realized what the off-world Stenaxes' true intentions were soon enough to prevent the future massacres.<ref name="TMS" />

==The conflict==
In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], during the month of 40:1 [[Great ReSynchronization|GrS]],<ref name="Date1" /> hundreds of Stenaxes attacked Imperials stationed on their worlds and killed them; they also fought non-military populations, and all non-Stenax inhabitants were soon dead. Not all Stenaxes who lived away from Stenos participated in the attacks, however. The Stenaxes seized deadly technology that the Galactic Empire had brought to Stenos and launched invasions of [[Kadril]], [[Arda II]], [[Sooma]], [[Alzar]], [[Tandankin]], and other worlds along the [[Gordian Reach (hyperspace route)|Gordian Reach]]. Millions died in the Stenax Massacres, with a bombardment reducing the planet Kadril to inhospitable rubble.<ref name="TMS" /> In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],<ref name="Date2">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates this event to the year after a raid on the Hast shipyards, which is dated to 4 ABY on the following page.</ref> the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], in spite of their decision to focus their efforts on defending the emerging government of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], left [[Calamari sector/Legends|Mon Calamari Space]] in force to bring the Stenaxes' campaign to an end.<ref name="TEA" /> New Republic forces [[Battle of Tharkos|defeated]] the Stenaxes at [[Tharkos]]<ref name="TMS" /> in 5 ABY.<ref name="TEA" />

==Aftermath==
The Stenax crews that survived were incarcerated, and [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] monitoring posts were constructed at Stenos to monitor their activities. Following the massacres, the New Republic closely watched the Stenaxes' access to advanced technology, and while the off-world Stenaxes who had not taken part in the attacks were allowed to continue living off of Stenos, [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] observed them from a distance. [[Historian/Legends|Historians]] would compare the massacres to the actions of the [[Yevetha]] during the [[Black Fleet Crisis]]. Many Stenaxes settled on [[Belderone/Legends|Belderone]] and [[Kulthis]], and based on their behavior there, sociologists cautioned that another event similar to the Stenax Massacres could happen.<ref name="TMS" />
